name: Sandhills Community Action Program, Inc.
description_short: HUD-approved non-profit providing free housing counseling, affordable housing assistance, and self-sufficiency programs across four North Carolina counties.
description_long:
Sandhills Community Action Program, Inc. is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that has served Anson, Montgomery, Moore, and Richmond counties in North Carolina for more than 50 years. The organization is part of a nationwide network of over 1,000 Community Action agencies designed to reduce poverty and help individuals achieve economic stability. Under Executive Director Nina Walker's leadership, SCAP operates four regional offices and focuses on empowering families through comprehensive support services.

SCAP offers a wide range of free and subsidized services including HUD-approved housing counseling, Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers, family self-sufficiency programs, home repair assistance, senior housing, and the S.T.A.R.S. self-sufficiency program focused on education, certifications, and employment support. They provide budgeting and credit management counseling as part of their housing counseling program and assist homeowners with major and minor repairs through programs funded by the NC Housing Finance Agency. The organization also administers federal rental subsidies and actively recruits landlords to expand housing options.

What distinguishes SCAP is its deep community roots and multi-service approach addressing housing, employment, and financial stability simultaneously. The organization requires that one-third of its Board of Directors consist of low-income individuals or their representatives, ensuring that the people they serve have direct voice in governance. They operate as a HUD-approved Housing Counseling Agency, providing legitimacy and federal recognition for their housing guidance services. Their four-county service area and multiple office locations ensure accessibility.

SCAP is best suited for low-to-moderate income individuals and families seeking free housing counseling, rental assistance, or home repair support. The organization genuinely provides free help rather than profit-driven services. However, their services are geographically limited to four North Carolina counties, and specific program eligibility requirements (income thresholds, property location, disability/age status for repair programs) may exclude some applicants. Those seeking credit repair or debt settlement should look elsewhere.
pros: HUD-approved Housing Counseling Agency providing legitimate, federally-recognized guidance on homebuying, renting, and housing needs
Administers Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers—the federal government's primary rental assistance program for very low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals
Offers free budgeting and credit management counseling as part of housing counseling services
S.T.A.R.S. program provides comprehensive support including education, certifications, employment assistance, and case management
Home repair programs (ESFR and URP) available for elderly homeowners, veterans, and those with disabilities in four counties
Board governance includes mandatory low-income representation ensuring accountability to the communities served
Part of national network of 1,000+ Community Action agencies providing advocacy and resource-sharing
cons: Services limited to four North Carolina counties (Anson, Montgomery, Moore, Richmond)—not available outside this geographic area
Home repair assistance has specific eligibility requirements (elderly, veteran, or disability status); not universal
Section 8 program likely has waiting lists given high demand for affordable housing vouchers
No credit repair or dispute services offered—strictly counseling and housing-focused programs
Website lacks specific program eligibility requirements, income limits, or application timelines, requiring direct contact for details
best_for: Low-income families seeking Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher assistance and rental subsidies
First-time homebuyers in four-county area needing free HUD-approved housing counseling
Elderly homeowners, veterans, and disabled individuals needing affordable home repair assistance in North Carolina
Individuals seeking free budgeting, credit management, and financial self-sufficiency coaching
diagnosis: Best for low-income residents of Anson, Montgomery, Moore, or Richmond counties in North Carolina seeking free housing counseling, rental assistance, or home repairs. Primary caveat: geographic service area is strictly limited to four North Carolina counties, and eligibility for repair programs requires specific status (elderly, veteran, or disability).
services: HUD-approved Housing Counseling (homebuying, renting, general housing needs)
Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher Program administration and rental subsidies
Family Self-Sufficiency Program (for Section 8 participants reducing welfare dependence)
S.T.A.R.S. Self-Sufficiency Program (education, certifications, employment support)
Home Repair—Emergency and Structural Facility Repair (ESFR) for eligible homeowners
Urgent Repair Program (URP) for minor repairs in Montgomery and Stanly counties
Affordable Senior Housing (1 and 2-bedroom apartments for age 62+)
Community Needs Assessment and community engagement programs
Landlord recruitment and partnership for rental properties
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
